channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: Google accidentally exposed details of unfixed Chromium flaw
item.description: Google has accidentally leaked details about an unfixed issue in Chromium that keeps JavaScript running in the background even when the browser is closed, allowing remote code execution on the device....
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: AI Agents Are Shifting Identity Security Budget Dynamics
item.description: AI agent projects are proliferating throughout the enterprise, and those AI agent identities require management, security, and governance. New Omdia research shows the AI agent identity budget dynamic...
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Apple blocked over $11 billion in App Store fraud in 6 years
item.description: Apple revealed that it blocked over $11 billion in fraudulent App Store transactions over the last six years, more than $2.2 billion in potentially fraudulent App Store transactions in 2025 alone. [.....
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Showboat Linux Malware Hits Middle East Telecom with SOCKS5 Proxy Backdoor
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new Linux malware dubbed Showboat that has been put to use in a campaign targeting a telecommunications provider in the Middle East since at least...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Inside a Crypto Drainer: How to Spot it Before it Empties Your Wallet
item.description: Modern crypto drainers don't hack wallets. They trick users into approving malicious transactions. Flare explores how the Lucifer DaaS platform scales wallet theft through phishing and automation. [....
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Chinese hackers target telcos with new Linux, Windows malware
item.description: A Chinese cyber-espionage campaign has been targeting telecommunications providers with newly discovered Linux and Windows malware dubbed Showboat and JFMBackdoor, respectively. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Chinese APTs Share Linux Backdoor in Central Asia Telco Attacks
item.description: "Showboat" doesn't show off, but clearly it doesn't need to, as it's long helped China spy on small market communications providers....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Max severity Cisco Secure Workload flaw gives Site Admin privileges
item.description: Cisco has released security updates to address a maximum-severity vulnerability in Secure Workload that allows attackers to gain Site Admin privileges. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Police seize “First VPN” service used in ransomware, data theft attacks
item.description: A virtual private network service called 'First VPN,' used in ransomware and data theft attacks, has been taken offline in a joint international law enforcement operation. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Content Delivery Exploit Opens Websites to Brand Hijacking
item.description: The Underminr domain-fronting attack allows threat actors to modify Web requests and leverage trusted websites to cloak malicious activity....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: Linux Rootkits, Router 0-Day, AI Intrusions, Scam Kits and 25 New Stories
item.description:
This week starts small.
A token leaks. A bad package slips in. A login trick works. An old tool shows up again. At first, it feels like the usual mess. Then you see the pattern: attackers are not alw...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Flipper One project needs community help to build open Linux platform
item.description: Flipper Devices, the maker of the Flipper Zero pentesting tool, is asking the community to help build Flipper One, an open Linux platform for connected devices. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft Warns of Two Actively Exploited Defender Vulnerabilities
item.description:
Microsoft has disclosed that a privilege escalation and a denial-of-service flaw in Defender has come under active exploitation in the wild.
The former, tracked as CVE-2026-41091, is rated 7.8 on the...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: When Identity is the Attack Path
item.description: Consider a cached access key on a single Windows machine. It got there the way most cached credentials do - a user logged in, and the key stored itself automatically. Standard AWS behavior. No one mis...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft warns of new Defender zero-days exploited in attacks
item.description: On Wednesday, Microsoft started rolling out security patches for two Defender vulnerabilities that have been exploited in zero-day attacks. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: 9-Year-Old Linux Kernel Flaw Enables Root Command Execution on Major Distros
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a vulnerability in the Linux kernel that remained undetected for nine years.
The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-46333 (CVSS score: 5.5), is a c...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: GitHub links repo breach to TanStack npm supply-chain attack
item.description: GitHub says the hackers who breached 3,800 internal repositories gained access via a malicious version of the Nx Console VS Code extension, compromised in last week's TanStack npm supply-chain attack....
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: GitHub Internal Repositories Breached via Malicious Nx Console VS Code Extension
item.description: GitHub on Wednesday officially confirmed that the breach of its internal repositories was the result of a compromise of an employee device involving a poisoned version of the Nx Console Microsoft Visu...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Highly Critical Drupal Core Flaw Exposes PostgreSQL Sites to RCE Attacks
item.description: Drupal has released security updates for a "highly critical" security vulnerability in Drupal Core that could be exploited by attackers to achieve remote code execution, privilege escalation, or infor...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Ukraine identifies infostealer operator tied to 28,000 stolen accounts
item.description: The Ukrainian cyberpolice, working in conjunction with U.S. law enforcement, has identified an 18-year-old man from Odesa suspected of running an infostealer malware operation targeting users of an on...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Hackers bypass SonicWall VPN MFA due to incomplete patching
item.description: Threat actors brute-forced VPN credentials and bypassed multi-factor authentication (MFA) on SonicWall Gen6 SSL-VPN appliances to deploy tools used in ransomware attacks. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Cyber Pros Can't Decide If AI Is a Good or a Bad Thing
item.description: There is nothing cybersecurity professionals are more excited about, and nothing they fear more, than AI....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: GitHub Confirms Breach, 4K Internal Repos Stolen
item.description: GitHub confirmed a data breach this week involving the theft of thousands of developer code repositories. One threat actor — TeamPCP — took credit....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Fake Android Apps Commit Carrier Billing Fraud for Premium Services
item.description: The disguised apps use WebView automation, JavaScript injection, and OTP interception to avoid detection and complete fraudulent subscriptions....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Processes and Culture Top Reasons Behind Data Breaches
item.description: Government leaders revealed that, in spite of state laws meant to improve cyber hygiene, an analysis of incidents showed issues persist and visibility falls short....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Microsoft Open-Sources RAMPART and Clarity to Secure AI Agents During Development
item.description:
Microsoft has unveiled two new open-source tools called RAMPART and Clarity to assist developers in better testing the security of artificial intelligence (AI) agents.
RAMPART, short for Risk Assessm...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Patch Now: Critical Flaw in OT Robot OS Gives Attackers Control
item.description: An unauthenticated attacker can exploit the command injection vulnerability to gain remote access to robotic systems, causing significant disruption to the environment....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Grafana breach caused by missed token rotation after TanStack attack
item.description: The Grafana data breach was caused by a single GitHub workflow token that slipped through the rotation process following the TanStack npm supply-chain attack last week. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer
